## Walkthrough — title, thesis, sequence

**Title**: *How Kerala's Christian sub-communities moved in 2026*

**Core thesis**: *Each Christian sub-rite has its own historical alliance pattern.*

**Sequence** (12 sections in 3 groups):

| # | Group | Section |
|---|---|---|
| §1 | Context | Kerala Christians: 18% of population, but politically diverse |
| §2 | Context | Each sub-rite lives in a distinct geographic cluster |
| §3 | Context | Each sub-rite has its own multi-cycle alliance pattern |
| §4 | Context | Syro-Malabar: UDF-leaning historically |

| §6 | Context | Indian Orthodox: perennial 50/50 |

| §9 | This election | 2026 Story B — Flips (Latin Catholic / Marthoma) |

| §11 | This election | Evidence the cohort effect is Christian-driven |

---

## §1 — Kerala Christians: 18% of population, but politically diverse

**Headline message**: Christians are ~18% of Kerala's population (down from ~24% in 1901; differential fertility + outmigration). But "Christian Kerala" is not one community — at least 9 distinct Christian denominations operate at AC granularity, each with its own theology, demographic geography, and political history. This walkthrough is a denomination-by-denomination read of what the 2026 election looked like.

**Data to cite**:
- Christian share of Kerala in 2025 projection: 17.6%
- Distinct Christian sub-rites with countable AC dominance: 6 cohorts (Latin / Syro-Malabar / Indian Orthodox / Jacobite / Marthoma / CSI)
- AC count where any Christian sub-rite is consequential: 79 of 140 (those clearing the 5%-of-voters threshold)

**Visualisation recommendation**:
- Statline header (no chart) — just the 17.6% number with sub-line listing the 6 sub-rite labels.
- Optional: small donut showing Hindu / Muslim / Christian / Other shares with Christian segment highlighted.

---

## §2 — Each sub-rite lives in a distinct geographic cluster

**Headline message**: The new OSM-derived sub-rite cohort layer (commit `42dee05`) gives the first AC-level view of where each Christian sub-community is most concentrated. Latin Catholic clusters along the coast (Trivandrum → Alleppey → Kochi → Kannur Latin coast). Syro-Malabar concentrates in central Kerala interior (Kottayam, Idukki, Ernakulam interior). Indian Orthodox is central Travancore (Tiruvalla / Niranam corridor). Smaller cohorts have tighter clusters.

**Data to cite**:
- Cohort sizes (AC counts, 5% voter-share threshold, ≥3 classified POIs):
  - Syro-Malabar: 31
  - Latin Catholic: 28
  - Indian Orthodox: 10
  - CSI: 5
  - Jacobite Syrian: 3
  - Marthoma: 2
  - No consequential Christian sub-rite: 61

**Visualisation recommendation**:
- **Categorical choropleth** — the dominant Christian sub-rite per AC, colour-coded.
- Reuse the existing `ReligionCategoricalMap` component (already on `/religion-map`).
- Adjacent legend with cohort counts.

---

## §3 — Each sub-rite has its own multi-cycle alliance pattern

**Headline message**: Looking at the past 4 cycles (2011 / 2016 / 2021 / 2026) by cohort, the cohorts move differently. Some have been UDF strongholds throughout; some have been LDF-leaning; some have oscillated. The 2026 swing only makes sense once you know each cohort's prior trajectory.

**Data to cite**: full cohort × year × alliance trajectory table (set up shape for §4-§7 deep-dives).

**Visualisation recommendation**:
- **Small-multiples line chart** — one mini panel per cohort, x = year (2011/2016/2021/2026), y = UDF vote share (and overlaid LDF as a secondary line).
- Use existing `TrajectoryLines` component (`src/components/charts/trajectory-lines.tsx`).
- All on the same y-scale (20% to 60%) for comparability.
- This is **the headline visualisation** of the walkthrough — the rest of the page is annotation.

---

## §4 — Syro-Malabar: UDF-leaning historically

**Headline message**: SM was UDF's most reliable Catholic base in 2011 (21 of 31 ACs UDF won). LDF clawed it close in 2016/2021 but didn't take it (13-14 wins). 2026 restored the 2011-era UDF dominance (26/31 wins).

**Data to cite**:

| Year | UDF | LDF | NDA | UDF wins / 31 |
|---|---:|---:|---:|:---:|
| 2011 | 48.7 | 44.3 | 5.1 | 21 |
| 2016 | 40.5 | 40.1 | 16.4 | 14 |
| 2021 | 40.2 | 43.8 | 11.8 | 13 |
| **2026** | **48.4** | **35.8** | **14.7** | **26** |

**Visualisation recommendation**:
- Emphasised version of the §3 line chart — show just the SM panel scaled larger, with the 2011 and 2026 values annotated to highlight the "return to baseline".
- A small inset showing the win count per cycle as a bar.

---

## §5 — Latin Catholic: structurally LDF-leaning 2011–2021

**Headline message**: This is the biggest reframe the new data enables. Latin Catholic ACs were **structurally LDF-leaning** through 2011–2021 (UDF won just 4 of 28 in both 2016 *and* 2021). The 2026 cycle flipped 17 of those 28 ACs LDF→UDF — the single largest single-cohort shift in any Christian sub-rite.

**Data to cite**:

| Year | UDF | LDF | NDA | UDF wins / 28 |
|---|---:|---:|---:|:---:|
| 2011 | 44.2 | 49.4 | 4.5 | 9 |
| 2016 | 36.2 | 47.8 | 14.3 | **4** |
| 2021 | 36.7 | 47.1 | 13.2 | **4** |
| **2026** | **45.6** | **39.7** | **13.7** | **21** |

**Visualisation recommendation**:
- Emphasised line chart highlighting the 4-cycle Latin Catholic trajectory.
- Annotate "2016/2021: UDF wins only 4 of 28" and "2026: UDF wins 21 of 28" as inline call-outs on the chart.

---

## §6 — Indian Orthodox: perennial 50/50

**Headline message**: Orthodox is **the perennial swing cohort**. Even in UDF's worst Christian year (2021), the alliance gap was only ~7pp. Even in UDF's best year (2026), Orthodox wins split 5/4/1. The Tiruvalla / Niranam / Aranmula corridor stays competitive across cycles in a way no other Christian sub-rite does.

**Data to cite**:

| Year | UDF | LDF | NDA | UDF wins / 10 |
|---|---:|---:|---:|:---:|
| 2011 | 47.9 | 45.4 | 5.0 | 5 |
| 2016 | 38.5 | 43.1 | 17.1 | 3 |
| 2021 | 37.6 | 44.4 | 16.5 | 2 |
| **2026** | **44.8** | **37.6** | **16.9** | **5** |

**Visualisation recommendation**:
- Emphasised line chart highlighting the consistently small gaps between UDF and LDF across all 4 cycles.
- Visually: this is the chart with the closest-together lines.

---

## §7 — Smaller cohorts: Jacobite, Marthoma, CSI

**Headline message**: Three smaller cohorts (n=3, 2, 5 ACs respectively) have distinct trajectories but carry only directional claims given the sample sizes. **Jacobite** stayed UDF-leaning historically and went 100% UDF in 2026 (3/3). **Marthoma** (n=2) flipped from LDF to UDF in 2026, similar to Latin Catholic. **CSI** is a 5-AC cohort that is *entirely* in Trivandrum district — its political behaviour is geographically confounded with Trivandrum's district-level patterns and shouldn't be read as denomination effect (caveat).

**Data to cite**:

| Cohort | n | UDF 2011 | UDF 2026 | UDF wins 2026 |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---|
| Jacobite Syrian | 3 | 49.5 | **52.1** | 3/0/0 |
| Marthoma | 2 | 45.9 | **44.5** | 2/0/0 |
| CSI | 5 | 36.2 | 35.4 | 2/2/1 |

**Visualisation recommendation**:
- Three small mini-trajectory panels side-by-side (continuing the §3 small-multiples template at smaller scale).
- For CSI panel, add a visible caveat banner: *"All 5 CSI cohort ACs are in Thiruvananthapuram district — cohort label confounded with district. Treat as a Trivandrum-area finding, not a CSI-denomination finding."*

---

## §8 — 2026 Story A: Restorations

**Headline message**: Three of the six Christian sub-rite cohorts — **Syro-Malabar, Jacobite, Indian Orthodox** — returned in 2026 to alliance levels they last saw in 2011, after a 2-cycle LDF-leaning wobble in 2016/2021. This is a **continuity story**: the 2026 cycle restored each cohort's long-run pattern, it didn't change it.

**Data to cite**:
- SM: 21 wins (2011) → 13 (2021) → 26 (2026). Wins 2026 ≈ 2011.
- Jacobite: 3/3 wins in 2011 → 1/3 in 2021 → 3/3 in 2026. Full restoration.
- Orthodox: 5/5/0 wins in 2011 → 2/8/0 in 2021 → 5/4/1 in 2026. Restored to 50/50.

**Visualisation recommendation**:
- **Annotated trajectory chart** — highlight the three "Restoration" cohorts (SM, Jacobite, Orthodox) on the §3 multi-cycle small-multiples, with a callout "see how each one bounces back to its 2011 level in 2026".

---

## §9 — 2026 Story B: Flips

**Headline message**: The other Christian sub-rite cohorts — **Latin Catholic and Marthoma** — show a genuinely different 2026 pattern. They didn't return to a UDF baseline — they **flipped** from LDF. Latin Catholic went from 4-of-28 UDF wins (2021) to 21-of-28 (2026). Marthoma went from 0-of-2 to 2-of-2. This is a **shift story** — the first time the new data lets us see this contrast cleanly.

**Data to cite**:
- Latin Catholic: 17 of 28 ACs flipped LDF→UDF between 2021 and 2026.
- Marthoma: 2 of 2 ACs flipped LDF→UDF (Aranmula, Ranni).

**Visualisation recommendation**:
- Mirror of §8 — same multi-cycle small-multiples, this time highlighting Latin Catholic + Marthoma with a different annotation: "these cohorts crossed parity for the first time in our dataset".
- Alternative: a "flip-count" bar chart showing AC-count-LDF→UDF per cohort.

---

## §10 — NDA hasn't succeeded with central Latin Catholics

**Headline message**: BJP/NDA gained ground in many Christian-cohort ACs in 2026, but the gains are geographically uneven within Latin Catholic. **Central Latin** (Ernakulam / Alappuzha / Kochi — where Latin Catholics are most concentrated) shows NDA share *shrinking* slightly between 2021–2026 (NDA 13% → 13%, Δ −0.4pp). South Latin (TVM coast) has structural NDA presence at 18%. North Latin (Kannur) is essentially NDA-free at 7.5%. The Latin Catholic heartland is the one zone where BJP's 2026 surge didn't land.

**Data to cite**:

| Latin Catholic zone | n | UDF 2026 | NDA 2026 | NDA Δ 2021→2026 |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| South (TVM + Kollam coastal) | 11 | 42.0 | **18.3** | +2.0 |
| Central (Alappuzha + Ernakulam + Thrissur) | 10 | **47.9** | **13.0** | −0.4 |
| North (Kannur + Kasaragod) | 7 | 48.1 | **7.5** | −0.5 |

**Visualisation recommendation**:
- **Three-panel zone chart** (south / central / north Latin Catholic), each showing 2021 vs 2026 NDA share. Highlight central panel as "no NDA progress here".
- Could also be a single grouped bar chart with NDA and UDF bars per zone.

---

## §11 — Evidence: the cohort effect is Christian-driven

**Headline message**: The cohort findings rest on **three independent pieces of evidence** that the effect is community-driven, not a confounder. (1) **Same-district control**: within Ernakulam district alone, Latin-cohort ACs swung +16.0pp UDF vs SM-cohort's +8.8pp — a 7pp gap that district context cannot explain. (2) **Dose-response by Christian share**: within every Christian cohort, ACs with higher Christian population swung +5pp more than those with lower — exactly the signature a Christian-driven swing would produce; non-Christian-cohort ACs show no such gradient. (3) **Hindu-NDA separation**: Hindu-belt NDA gains in 2026 happened in Hindu-majority ACs (Thrissur Hindu ACs NDA +10.8pp), but bled essentially nothing into Christian-cohort ACs (Thrissur SM ACs NDA −0.9pp).

**Data to cite**:

| Test | Christian-cohort result | Comparison / control |
|---|---|---|
| M1 — same-district Ernakulam | Latin +16.0pp UDF | SM (same district) +8.8pp |
| M2 — Christian-share dose-response | High-share Latin/SM/Orthodox: +8 to +11pp | Below-threshold: no Christian-share gradient |
| M3 — Hindu-NDA bleed | Thrissur SM NDA −0.9pp | Thrissur Hindu ACs NDA +10.8pp |

**Visualisation recommendation**:
- **Three-panel evidence card** — one mini chart per test, showing the comparison clearly.
- For M1: bar chart with two bars (Latin Ernakulam +16.0, SM Ernakulam +8.8).
- For M2: scatter or paired-bar showing high vs low Christian share within each cohort.
- For M3: bar showing Thrissur SM vs Thrissur Hindu NDA Δ.

---

## §12 — Methodology: how we ruled out confounding variables

**Headline message**: The cohort framework was checked for three classic confounders. Each was tested independently; all three came back supporting the cohort claim. This section documents *how* the tests were run, in case the reader wants to audit.

### Concern 1 — Hindu-NDA absorption

> "Maybe Christians swung +10pp everywhere, but in south Latin, Hindu co-residents shifted to NDA, lowering the AC-level swing."

**How tested**: computed NDA Δ alongside UDF Δ per Latin Catholic geographic zone (south/central/north). If south Latin showed disproportionate NDA growth, Hindu absorption is supported.

**Result**: south Latin NDA grew +2.0pp (modest). Central and north Latin NDA shrank −0.4 and −0.5pp. The +2.0 in south accounts for ~2pp of the 3-4pp swing gap; the rest is genuinely a smaller Christian swing.

**Conclusion**: Hindu-NDA absorption is real in south Latin but small in magnitude. Doesn't dominate the cohort signal.

### Concern 2 — District confounding

> "Maybe SM is just 'Kottayam-Idukki swung this way' and Latin is just 'coast swung that way' — sub-rite is incidental."

**How tested**: restricted to ACs within a single district (Ernakulam) that contains both SM-cohort and Latin-cohort ACs. If both swung the same amount, district was the variable. If they swung differently, the cohort label carries information.

**Result**: within Ernakulam, Latin-cohort ACs swung +16.0pp UDF vs SM-cohort's +8.8pp. A 7pp same-district gap.

**Conclusion**: cohort identity carries information beyond district. District is not the confounder.

### Concern 3 — Within-cohort heterogeneity by Christian share

> "Maybe a Latin-cohort AC at 10% Christian and one at 41% Christian aren't really the same thing — the 10% one's swing is driven by non-Christians."

**How tested**: split each cohort at its median Christian share. Compared the swing in the high-share half vs the low-share half. If high-share ACs swung more, the cohort signal is Christian-driven (dose-response). If they swung similarly, religion-share doesn't matter.

**Result**: every Christian cohort shows a positive high-vs-low Christian-share gap of +2 to +5pp ΔUDF. The non-Christian below-threshold cohort shows no gradient (slightly *negative*). Dose-response pattern.

**Conclusion**: cohort effect intensifies with Christian share. Strong signature of a Christian-driven effect. Community-level inference is most defensible in the high-share subset.

### What we cannot rule out

- **Pre-2011 baseline**: our historical data starts at 2011. "Structurally LDF-leaning Latin Catholic 2011–2021" is a 3-cycle observation, not a 5-cycle one. Pre-2011 patterns could differ.
- **Individual-level inference**: we have no exit poll or CSDS microdata. All community-level claims rest on the AC-aggregate-to-community structural assumption.
- **Small-cohort n**: Jacobite (n=3), Marthoma (n=2), CSI (n=5) carry directional claims only.

**Visualisation recommendation**:
- Mostly a text-heavy methodology section.
- Include the three small evidence tables (one per concern) and the data behind each test.
- This is a "how we know what we know" page — design for clarity over polish.

---

## Key insights (curated summary, for quick scan)

- **Sub-rite identity is a second-order variable.** Religion population share predicts UDF *level* better than sub-rite identity does. But sub-rite tracks *historical alignment* and *multi-cycle trajectory* — that's where the new data adds value.
- **The cohort effect is not a district effect in disguise** (M2). Within Ernakulam district, Latin-cohort ACs swung +16.0pp UDF while SM-cohort ACs swung +8.8pp — a 7pp same-district gap.
- **The cohort effect is Christian-driven, not aggregate noise** (M3). Within every Christian cohort, high-Christian-share ACs swung +5pp more than low-Christian-share ACs in the same cohort. Below-threshold (non-Christian-cohort) ACs show no such gradient.
- **Latin Catholic was structurally LDF-leaning 2011–2021** (just 4 of 28 UDF wins in 2016 AND 2021), then flipped in 2026 (21 of 28). The biggest single sub-rite shift of 2026.
- **Syro-Malabar has been UDF's most reliable Catholic base.** 21 wins in 2011, LDF wobble in 2016/2021, 26 in 2026 — the textbook return-to-baseline story.
- **Indian Orthodox is the perennial swing** — always close to 50/50; even in UDF's best 2026, wins split 5/4/1.
- **CSI cohort is really a Trivandrum-geographic cluster, not a denomination effect.** All 5 CSI cohort ACs are in Thiruvananthapuram district.
- **NDA hasn't succeeded with central Latin Catholics** — Ernakulam/Alappuzha/Kochi Latin ACs show NDA share flat or shrinking in 2026 while UDF surged. The Latin heartland is the BJP-resistance zone.

---
